name: Diamond / Rhombus
description: A rhombic prism shape — essentially a 3D diamond or double pyramid. Dark grey to near-black metallic sheen. No lights, no markings, no sound. Defined almost entirely by its geometric silhouette against the sky. The Calvine photograph (1990) is the canonical case: a roughly 30-meter dark-grey diamond hanging in the Scottish sky alongside a Harrier jet. Vertical-only departure — straight up at high speed with no banking or turning.

ir_signature.ir_notes: No IR footage exists for canonical Calvine case. Photographic evidence only: color prints showing dark metallic sheen.

cultural_context: The Calvine photograph, taken August 4 1990 near Calvine, Scotland, is considered one of the most significant UFO photographs ever taken by British MoD analysts who studied it. Six color prints were confiscated; the sole remaining print held by RAF press officer Craig Lindsay was published in August 2022 via Sheffield Hallam University — 32 years after the event. Nick Pope, who ran the MoD UFO desk, called it the best UFO photograph he'd ever seen.
